Package org.rodinp.core

Interface IAttributeValue

All Known Subinterfaces:
IAttributeValue.Boolean, IAttributeValue.Handle, IAttributeValue.Integer, IAttributeValue.Long, IAttributeValue.String
All Known Implementing Classes:
AttributeValue, AttributeValue.Boolean, AttributeValue.Handle, AttributeValue.Integer, AttributeValue.Long, AttributeValue.String
public interface IAttributeValue
Common protocol for manipulating attribute values in a generic way.

Attributes are attached to internal elements (that is elements that are stored in a file element) and provide non-structural information pertaining to that element (for instance the target of a refines clause).

Every attribute value contains an attribute type and an object specifying the actual value. Attribute values are immutable objects. The correspondence between the attribute type and the actual class of the value instance is given by the following table.

Attribute type Value type
IAttributeType.Boolean Boolean
IAttributeType.Handle IRodinElement
IAttributeType.Integer Integer
IAttributeType.Long Long
IAttributeType.String String

Instances of attribute values can be created either from their corresponding type using methods makeValue() or obtained from the database using IInternalElement.getAttributeValues().

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IAttributeType.Boolean.makeValue(boolean)
  • IAttributeType.Handle.makeValue(IRodinElement)
  • IAttributeType.Integer.makeValue(int)
  • IAttributeType.Long.makeValue(long)
  • IAttributeType.String.makeValue(java.lang.String)
  • IInternalElement.getAttributeValues()
  • IInternalElement.setAttributeValue(IAttributeValue, IProgressMonitor)

    equals(Object obj)
    Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one.
    IAttributeType

    Returns the value of this attribute value.

  • Method Details

    • getType

      IAttributeType getType()
      Returns the type of this attribute value.
      Returns:
      the type of this attribute value

    • getValue

      Object getValue()
      Returns the value of this attribute value.
      Returns:
      the value of this attribute value

    • equals

      boolean equals(Object obj)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one.

      Two attribute values are considered equal iff they contain the same attribute type and their values are equal.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - the reference object with which to compare.
      Returns:
      true iff this object and the given object are equal attribute values
      See Also:
      • Object.equals(Object)

    • hashCode

      int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this attribute value, compatible with equality as defined by equals(Object).
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this attribute value
